text: The surgeon is performing a procedure on the round ligament on the right side, using robotic instruments.
The surgeon uses a bipolar Maryland forceps on the left side and a robotic EndoWrist instrument on the right side to manipulate tissues during the robotic hysterectomy.
The surgeon opens the anterior leaf of the broad ligament using robotic instruments, revealing underlying anatomical structures.

The surgeon uses monopolar scissors to separate the UV fold and the bladder. The right uterine artery is coagulated and cut with scissors. The fallopian tube and ovarian ligament are visible, and the procedure is now focused on the left side.

The surgeon cuts the left side of the round ligament and separates the left fallopian tube and ovarian ligament. The posterior peritoneum is then separated up to the cervix at the junction with the uterosacral ligament.
The surgeon uses a surgical instrument to coagulate the left uterine artery as medially as possible before cutting it.
The surgeon inserts the full circle colpotomizer and performs a colpotomy at the level of the tip of the colpotomizer, creating an incision around the cervix.
The surgeon performs a posterior colpotomy, following the completion of the anterior colpotomy, using surgical instruments to incise and separate the posterior vaginal wall.
The uterus is extracted through the vaginal canal and temporarily positioned within the vagina to prevent gas leakage. The surgeon uses a bidirectional barbed suture to close the vaginal vault.
